title: Task count summary in list output

story.i_want_to: see a summary line at the bottom of the task list showing counts
story.so_that: I can quickly see how many tasks are pending vs done at a glance

rationale: A summary line adds polish and utility without complexity. It gives users instant insight into their overall progress without having to count rows manually.

acceptance_criteria.description: After listing tasks, a summary line is printed with correct singular/plural forms: '1 task (1 pending, 0 done)' or '5 tasks (3 pending, 2 done)'.
The summary reflects the current filter: if --pending is used, it says 'Showing {n} pending task(s) ({total} total)'; if --done is used, it says 'Showing {n} completed task(s) ({total} total)'.
The summary line is visually separated from the task table (blank line before it).
When there are no tasks at all, the empty-state message is shown instead of a summary ('No tasks yet. Add one with: todo add "your task"'). No summary line is printed for empty state.

test_cases.name: Summary shows correct counts for mixed list
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing 3 pending and 2 done tasks

test_cases.name: Summary adapts to --pending filter
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing 3 pending and 2 done tasks

test_cases.name: Summary adapts to --done filter
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing 3 pending and 2 done tasks

test_cases.name: Singular form for one task
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing exactly 1 pending task
expected_results: stdout contains '1 task (1 pending, 0 done)' (singular 'task', not 'tasks')

test_cases.name: Empty list shows friendly message, no summary
Filtered empty result shows message, no summary
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing only pending tasks
expected_results: stdout does NOT contain '0 completed tasks'

technical_notes: Count tasks using list comprehension: sum(1 for t in tasks if t['status'] == 'pending')
Print summary after the table, separated by a blank line
Use helper for pluralization: f"{n} {'task' if n == 1 else 'tasks'}"
When filter is active, show both filtered count and total count for context
Skip summary entirely for empty state (no tasks) and filtered empty state (no matching tasks)
